threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the tube instead of the underground; locals have no idea what you mean when you say 'the Central Line.'
The 59 bus driver last night stopped for a pigeon trying to cross Coldharbour Road. He just sighed and let it waddle across!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid on a scooter dodging a pigeon while his mom chatted with a neighbor over coffee outside the Spätkauf. Definitely home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find techno parties that start on a Monday morning and last until Tuesday's sunrise, with folks emerging from the U-Bahn like it's just another weekend.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they walk past the BART crowds without flinching, while the newcomers gawk and panic at the delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 at 6 PM yesterday, packed shoulder to shoulder. Someone got off at Market and forgot their burrito—hilarious chaos as it rolled down the steps.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every plaza is for leisure, but locals know that Plaça del Sol turns into a makeshift bar during the late nights. No quiet spots here!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their overly enthusiastic use of 'Hola!'—it's like every interaction is an audition for a friendly local role.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still don’t know the unwritten rule of not wearing your backpack on the bus. You’re a walking target for side-eye.
Saw a guy on the 59 last night doing magic tricks for a group of unsuspecting passengers. The look on their faces was priceless!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a couple arguing in front of the Späti over the last pack of Zigaretten, both too stubborn to back down. Just another Tuesday in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a late-night Döner spot packed with techno music and a line that wraps around the block. It's a unique blend of food and culture.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to navigate around BART delays—only tourists blame the Muni for late arrivals.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy trying to charge his phone in the Muni bus with a 9-volt battery. Classic Tenderloin ingenuity, but also a little concerning.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for hipster cafés, but the real charm is in the hidden plazas where locals gather, sharing life under the trees.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their bewildered expression when a bus driver yells at them for not knowing to swipe their card at the machine first.
